dp[mask] = sum of elements in mask. Valid if bucket sums work modulo target. O(n × 2^n).
dp[mask][i] = shortest string using words in mask, ending with word i. Precompute overlaps between all pairs.
Bitmask tracks chosen numbers. dp[mask] = can current player force a win? Minimax with memoization.
Bitmask tracks completed tasks. dp[mask] = min time to complete tasks in mask respecting dependencies.

key_points: dp[n] = Σ dp[i-1] × dp[n-i] for i = 1..n
Catalan sequence: 1, 1, 2, 5, 14, 42, ...

structural_signals.why_explanation: Each root i splits into left (i-1 nodes) × right (n-i nodes). dp[n] = Σ dp[i-1]×dp[n-i]. Catalan numbers.
Same recurrence but build actual trees. For each root, recursively generate all left and right subtrees.
Catalan number C(n) = number of valid arrangements of n pairs. Same recurrence as unique BSTs.
Full binary tree with n leaves: Catalan(n-1). Each decomposition = left subtree × right subtree.

key_points: Return value bottom-up: gain per subtree for the parent
Track global answer via closure variable (can fork at any node)
Can't fork when reporting upward — must pick one branch

structural_signals.why_explanation: At each node: pathThrough = val+leftGain+rightGain (both branches). Return val+max(left,right) upward (one branch only).
Same pattern: left+right at each node = path through it. Track global max. Return max(left,right)+1 upward.
Return (rob, skip) per node. rob = val + skip(children). skip = max(rob, skip) per child. DP on tree structure.
DP on tree: each node tracks best path going left vs going right. Swap direction at each level.

key_points: Dungeon works backwards — compute minimum health needed, not gained
Cherry pickup uses two simultaneous paths
Reduce dimensions where possible (e.g., 4D → 3D)

structural_signals.why_explanation: Work BACKWARDS: dp[i][j] = min health needed entering (i,j). Forward doesn't work because you need to survive every step.
Two paths simultaneously: dp[r1][c1][r2], c2 derived. Both collectors move in sync, share cherries at same cell.
Two robots from top corners, moving down. dp[row][c1][c2] = max cherries. 3D DP, O(m×n²).
dp[steps][r][c] = number of paths that exit grid. 3D DP with modular arithmetic.

key_points: Fibonacci naive is O(2^n) —always memoizeor use tail recursion
Fast power halves exponent each step — O(log n) multiplications

structural_signals.why_explanation: n! = n × (n-1)!. Direct recursion with base case n≤1. Foundation for permutation counting.
Two-branch recursion: fib(n) = fib(n-1) + fib(n-2). MUST memoize — naive is O(2^n).
Fast power: x^n = (x^(n/2))². Halves exponent → O(log n). Used in cryptography, competitive programming.
Move n-1 to aux, move largest to target, move n-1 from aux to target. 2^n - 1 moves. Classic recursion demo.

key_points: String recursion shrinks by removing characters from front or both ends
Subsequences = include/exclude each char =2^n results

structural_signals.why_explanation: reverse(s) = reverse(s[1..]) + s[0]. Base case: empty or single char. O(n) calls, O(n) stack.
Compare s[left] with s[right], recurse inward. Base case: left ≥ right → true. Converging recursion.
At each char: include it or exclude it. Two branches → 2^n total subsequences. Classic include/exclude recursion.
Fix each char at position 0, recursively permute the rest. n! results.

key_points: Recursion unwinds fromtail to head— builds result on return trip
Recursive reversal uses O(n) stack — preferiterative for large lists

structural_signals.why_explanation: Recurse to tail, then rewire: head.next.next = head, head.next = null. Builds reversed list on unwind. O(n) stack.
Pick smaller head, recurse on remaining. Base case: either list null. Natural recursive structure.
Recurse to end, on return: skip matching nodes. head.next = removeElements(head.next, val).
Recurse to end, compare with front pointer on unwind. Front advances forward, recursion unwinds backward.

key_points: Trees are recursion'snatural home— each subtree is a subproblem
Pattern 1:return bottom-up(height, count, LCA)
Pattern 2:pass info top-down(BST bounds, path sum target)

structural_signals.why_explanation: Bottom-up: max(left, right) + 1. The simplest tree recursion. Base case: null → 0.
1 + count(left) + count(right). Same bottom-up pattern, different combine operation.
Compare root values, then recurse on both subtrees. Two-tree recursion.
LCA: bottom-up (check both subtrees). BST valid: top-down (pass min/max bounds). Two fundamental patterns.
Inorder/preorder/postorder = when you process root relative to children. Recursion makes this trivial.

key_points: Three steps:divide → conquer → combine
Subproblems areINDEPENDENT(unlike DP where they overlap)
Merge sort is the canonical example — O(n log n) guaranteed

structural_signals.why_explanation: Split in half, sort each, merge. O(n log n) guaranteed. The canonical divide-and-conquer algorithm.
Partition around pivot, recurse on each side. O(n log n) average. In-place but O(n²) worst case.
Modified merge sort: count cross-inversions during merge step. O(n log n) — much better than O(n²) brute force.
Divide points by x-coordinate, solve each half, check strip. O(n log n).
Max subarray is in left half, right half, or crosses the midpoint. Combine by checking crossing sum.

key_points: Transforms O(2^n) to O(n) by caching results of subproblems
Check cache first, then compute — avoid redundant work
UsegetOrPutpattern in Kotlin for clean memoization

structural_signals.why_explanation: Same subproblems computed many times. Cache fib(k) → each computed once. O(2^n) → O(n).
memo[amount] = min coins. Try each coin, recurse on amount-coin. Without memo → exponential.
memo[i][j] = LCS of s1[i..] and s2[j..]. Two indices → 2D cache. O(m×n) with memo.
If the same arguments appear multiple times in the recursion tree → add cache. Universal optimization.

key_points: Include/exclude each element =2^n subsets
Sort + skip for duplicates:if (i > start && nums[i] == nums[i-1]) continue
Bitmask alternative: iterate masks 0..2^n-1

structural_signals.why_explanation: Include/exclude each element. Record at EVERY node (not just leaves). 2^n results. Loop from start.
Sort first, then skip same-value elements at the same decision level: if (i > start && nums[i] == nums[i-1]) continue.
Any problem asking "all possible selections" — feature selection, test case generation, set cover.
Alternative to recursion: iterate mask 0..2^n-1. Bit i set → include nums[i]. Same 2^n results, iterative.

key_points: Loop from0 with used[](not from start like subsets)
Sort + skip unused duplicates:nums[i] == nums[i-1] && !used[i-1]
Swap-based permutation is in-place but harder to handle duplicates

structural_signals.why_explanation: Order matters, use all elements. Loop from 0 with used[] array. Record at LEAVES (when cur.size == n). n! results.
Sort + skip: if (nums[i] == nums[i-1] && !used[i-1]) continue. Prevents duplicate orderings.
Not backtracking — use the algorithmic approach (find rightmost ascent, swap, reverse suffix). O(n).
Permutations of characters in a string. Use permutations with duplicates to avoid repeated anagrams.
Don't generate all n! — use factorial number system to directly compute the kth permutation in O(n²).

key_points: Start index controls reuse:i+1 = no reuse,i = reuse allowed
Prune whencandidate > remaining(sort input first)

structural_signals.why_explanation: Choose k items from n, order doesn't matter. Loop from start, record when size == k. C(n,k) results.
Find combos summing to target, each element reusable. Recurse with i (not i+1). Sort + prune when candidate > remaining.
Same but each element used once. Recurse with i+1. Sort + skip duplicates at same level.
Choose k numbers from 1-9 summing to n. Fixed-size combination with target constraint.
Each digit maps to 3-4 letters. At each digit position, try all mapped letters. Product of choices.

key_points: Constraint: can add'('ifopen < n, can add')'ifclose < open
Number of results = Catalan number C(n)

structural_signals.why_explanation: Build valid combos of n pairs. Add '(' if open < n, ')' if close < open. Catalan(n) results.
Any problem generating all valid nested/balanced structures — HTML tags, bracket expressions.
Catalan number counts both valid parenthesizations and unique BST shapes. Same recurrence.

key_points: Place row by row, trackcols + diag1(row-col) + diag2(row+col)
Pruning eliminates99.9%of search space

structural_signals.why_explanation: Place n queens so none attack. Row-by-row placement with col/diag1/diag2 constraint sets. O(n!) with pruning.
Same algorithm but just increment counter instead of building board strings. Slightly faster.
Any placement problem with row/column/diagonal constraints — bishops, rooks, knights on a board.

key_points: Try digits 1–9 in each empty cell,backtrack on invalid
Optimization: fillmost-constrained cell first(MRV heuristic)

structural_signals.why_explanation: Try digits 1-9 in each empty cell, check row/col/box validity, backtrack on failure. Template 2 (find ANY solution).
Same pattern: try words in each slot, check constraints (intersecting letters), backtrack on conflict.
Any fill-in puzzle with row/column/region rules. MRV heuristic (most-constrained cell first) dramatically speeds up.

key_points: Mark cell visited before recursing,RESTORE after(backtrack)
4-directional exploration using direction array

Build Trie from word list, DFS the board following Trie edges. Prune branches not in Trie.

Find all paths from start to end. Mark visited, try 4 directions, backtrack. Collect valid paths.
